description:
Add crates/balls-github-shared/ holding the code both plugins will share:
the GitHub HTTP client (github.rs), auth-dir-parameterized token I/O
(auth.rs), the base config types reused by both plugins (config.rs split
into shared base + per-plugin extensions), error types (error.rs), and the
generic Task/SyncReport types used over the plugin protocol (types.rs).
Forge plugin imports from the shared crate.

WHY: the shared lib is what makes "two binaries, one workspace" worth more
than "two separate repos". It's also what enforces the participant model's
disjoint-projection property at the code level: the shared lib MUST NOT
declare or own anything under external.github.* or external.github_issues.*
— those projections live in each plugin's own crate. Auth-dir is parameter,
not hardcoded, so each binary points at its own directory per the Epic-B
separate-token decision.

ACCEPTANCE:
- crates/balls-github-shared/ exists with src/{auth,github,config_base,error,
  types}.rs (or equivalent module shape). Each file under the 300-line cap.
- balls-plugin-github depends on balls-github-shared via path; no duplicated
  HTTP/auth/error code remains in the binary crate.
- Forge plugin's existing tests pass unchanged. Coverage 100% across the
  workspace.
- The shared crate has zero references to external.github.* or
  external.github_issues.* — verified by a test that grep-asserts this
  property holds (a small ci-style guard against future regression).
- auth-dir is fully parameterized: shared lib never hardcodes a path; the
  binary picks `.balls/local/plugins/github/`.
title: Extract balls-github-shared crate (auth, http, config, error, types)
